content.problemStatement:
Andy and Bob are the only two delivery men of Pizza-chef store. Today, the store received **N** orders. 
It's known that the amount of tips may be different when handled by different delivery man. 
More specifically, if Andy takes the **i**th order, he would be tipped **Ai** dollars and if Bob takes this order, 
the tip would be **Bi** dollars. 



They decided that they would distribute the orders among themselves to maximize the total tip money. One order will be handled by only
one person. Also, due to time constraints Andy cannot take more than **X** orders and Bob cannot take more than 
**Y** orders. It is guaranteed that **X** + **Y** is greater than or equal to **N**, which means that all the orders can be handled 
by either Andy or Bob.



Please find out the maximum possible amount of total tip money after processing all the orders.
content.inputFormat: First line contains three integers N, X, Y. The second line contains N integers. The ith integer represents Ai. The third line contains N integers. The ith integer represents Bi.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer representing the maximum tip money they would receive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Bob will take the first three orders and Andy will take the rest.
Andy will take the first two orders and Bob will take the last order.
Andy will take the first two orders and Bob will take the last two orders.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 3D DP array where dp[i][j][k] represents the maximum tip money that can be received after processing the first i orders, with j orders taken by Andy and k orders taken by Bob. We fill up the DP array by considering all possible cases and taking the maximum tip money.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * X * Y) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * X * Y) for the DP array
